subject: Physics
title: The Fermi Paradox
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Fermi Paradox.

hook.text: The universe has billions of stars with planets. So where is everybody? Enrico Fermi asked this at lunch in 1950.

buildup.text: There are 200 billion trillion stars in the observable universe. Many have planets in habitable zones.

discovery.text: Even with slow space travel, a civilization could colonize the galaxy in a few million years—a blink in cosmic time.

twist.text: Yet we've found zero evidence of alien civilizations. No signals, no probes, no megastructures. Just silence.

climax.text: Possible explanations range from the Great Filter to the Zoo Hypothesis to the simple answer: we're alone.

punchline.text: Billions of worlds. Zero visitors. The silence is deafening.

quiz.question: What is the Fermi Paradox?
options: The contradiction between the high probability of alien life and the lack of evidence
The mystery of why the universe is expanding
The impossibility of traveling faster than light
